_metadata.note: One count per coded passage per speech. A passage receiving two stacked codes counts once per code. D2 codes are not applied to speeches in v3 — they belong to the formal documentation corpus (Corpus B).

v3_vs_v2_changes.detail: Five formerly-D2 passages re-coded as D1.1 > Rejection_of_Multilateral_Governance (3), D1.1 > Rejection_of_Multilateral_Governance + D1.1 > Sustainability_as_Ideological_or_Harmful (1 passage = 2 codes), D1.1 > Deregulation_Benefits + D1.1 > Anti_State_Anti_Bureaucratic (1 passage). Five D2 codes removed (4x D2.3, 1x D2.2). Temporal_Appropriation count increased from 1 to 3 as UN 2025 passages are now both coded as INDUCTIVE > Temporal_Appropriation rather than D2.3.

key_analytical_observations.dominant_mechanism: D1.1 (State/Governance) is the dominant rhetorical register at 55% of codes, confirming that the primary delegitimation target is state authority over sustainability governance, not just the scientific consensus.
key_analytical_observations.Rejection_of_Multilateral_Governance: The single most frequent code (12), reflecting that all five post-debate speeches were delivered at international forums. V3 increase from v2 (was 9) reflects re-coding of formerly-D2 institutional announcement passages as D1.1.
key_analytical_observations.Temporal_Appropriation: Increased to 3 corpus instances (from 1 in v2). The two UN 2025 passages that were D2.3 in v2 reveal their Temporal_Appropriation character once freed from the D2 frame. Three instances exceed the informal threshold for formalizing this as a permanent deductive sub-code.
key_analytical_observations.D1_3_cultural_backlash: At 26% of codes, cultural backlash is substantial but not dominant. The civilizational frame (7 instances, across all three Davos speeches and both UN speeches) functions as a meta-narrative rather than a frequently deployed specific argument.
key_analytical_observations.D1_2_science_expertise: At 18%, public science/expertise delegitimization is less frequent than governance delegitimization — consistent with the theoretical framing that libertarian obstruction targets state authority more than climate science per se.
key_analytical_observations.no_D2_in_speeches_confirmed: Zero D2 codes in v3 corpus. The re-coded passages all carry richer D1 analytical content, confirming that the v2 D2 codes were misclassifying rhetorical acts as institutional acts.
